shape_decode: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = _,L,W,_ : value_profile_cmplog RESOLVES, value_profile BLOCKS, cmplog+naive non-decisive -> i2s-pro (vpc's I2S lands the exact multibyte literal / assembles the exact structure; vp's Hamming-prefix gradient stalls before it).
round_note: FINAL re-design. Round-3 status from the re-invoke brief: all 3 branches are rule_not_met (scored on the owning server sB, none held) -- NOT seed_starved (the prior round's seed_starved framing for 8138/9435 is SUPERSEDED; sB did score them). All 3 prior hypotheses are carried forward verbatim per the superset/monotonic rule. NEW discriminating mechanisms are added for 6193 and 9435 using BUILT-but-not-yet-tried tools (depth_reach, corpus_size_ratio). 8138 is judged honestly decidable:false (decode-derived value with no corpus-content signature for any built tool).

hypotheses.label: vpc's I2S enriches the exact multibyte literal in its corpus; value_profile's gradient does not

arm_repointing.note: Repoint operand_enrichment's enrichment baseline from its default cmplog-vs-naive to vpc(I2S-winner)-vs-value_profile(VP-blind-loser). Param repointing of the SAME byte_freq_ratio measurement at the W/L gate offsets, not a new tool.

target_literals.libxml2_6193: xmlns\0 (6-byte contiguous_literal at the SAX2.c:1170-1171 equality chain)

hypotheses.prediction: G1: i2s-pro predicts the exact multibyte target literal is ENRICHED in the vpc-resolving corpus at the gate offset and signed_target_enrich > 0, while value_profile (gradient-blind to the exact multibyte sequence) does NOT enrich it. Discriminated ALTERNATIVE: if value_profile's Hamming gradient could climb to the literal, the literal would appear in the VP corpus too and signed_target_enrich would be ~0 or negative.
hypotheses.status_note: KEEP / carry-forward per superset rule. Prior hypothesis verbatim (rule unchanged, no relaxed threshold). Returned rule_not_met for 6193 (the fixed-offset byte_freq_ratio model is mismatched: the gate compares a derived name[] buffer from xmlSplitQName, not a raw byte at a stable input offset). Retained UNCHANGED so any other server / future libxml2 corpus re-scores it; tried LAST in decision_order.

hypotheses.label: vpc's I2S lands AT the exact multibyte literal; value_profile's Hamming gradient stalls a measurable distance short of it

arm_repointing.note: The shape's decisive pair is vpc(W) > vp(L). value_distance_reached natively computes per-arm min sliding-window Hamming distance to operand_literal for all four arms; the discriminating comparison here is vpc_min_distance (lands at literal) vs vp_min_distance (gradient stalls). winner_closer is evaluated on vpc-vs-vp, not vp-vs-cmp.

hypotheses.prediction: G1 (genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism than the byte_freq_ratio enrichment): the i2s-pro / gradient-blind story predicts vpc's seeds reach the exact 6-byte 'xmlns\0' literal (vpc_min_distance ~ 0) while value_profile's prefix-Hamming gradient stalls a measurable distance short of the NUL-terminated 6-way equality chain (vp_min_distance substantially > 0), so winner_closer=true and distance_gap is large. DISCRIMINATED ALTERNATIVE: that value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient actually DOES climb to within a byte of the literal (so vpc has no real distance advantage) -- if so vp_min_distance ~ 0, distance_gap ~ 0, winner_closer=false. Uses a SLIDING WINDOW (no fixed-offset assumption), so it survives the derived-name[] / xmlSplitQName-output offset problem that made the enrichment test mismatched.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.2 AND vpc_min_distance <= 0.05
hypotheses.status_note: KEEP / carry-forward per superset rule. Verbatim rule, no relaxed threshold. Returned rule_not_met at round-2/3 scoring (the literal materializes in a DERIVED name[] buffer from xmlSplitQName, so a raw-window distance can miss it). Retained UNCHANGED; tried THIRD in decision_order.
hypotheses.caveat: operand width 6 is above the width<=2 chance-alignment flag. value_distance_reached reads on-disk seeds; libxml2 has no on-disk corpus on s4 -- the OWNING server (sB holds libxml2) scores this. Specified for ALL servers per cross-server rule.

hypotheses.label: the 'xmlns' attribute-name token appears in vpc's resolving corpus but is absent/depleted in value_profile's blocking corpus

arm_repointing.note: joint_necessity natively computes a per-arm structural-TOKEN count (vp_tags / cmp_tags / vpc_tags) over the vpc-RESOLVING vs value_profile-BLOCKING seed sets via the --tokens knob. Here --tokens is repointed from font/SQL tags to the single XML token 'xmlns'. Discriminating metrics: vpc_tags vs vp_tags and their ratio tag_lift. size_lift is auxiliary context, NOT in the rule.

hypotheses.prediction: G1: the i2s-pro story predicts the literal 'xmlns' token is PRESENT in vpc's resolving corpus (vpc_tags >= 1 per resolving seed) because I2SRandReplace splices the logged per-character 'xmlns' comparands into the input, while value_profile's gradient-only search cannot synthesize the contiguous token (vp_tags ~ 0). OFFSET-FREE (counts the token anywhere in the seed). DISCRIMINATED ALTERNATIVE: if value_profile actually DOES land the 'xmlns' token via its gradient, vp_tags ~ vpc_tags and tag_lift ~ 1, REFUTING the gradient-blind story.
hypotheses.status_note: KEEP / carry-forward per superset rule (added in the prior round; the brief marks it confirmed >=1 branch). Verbatim rule, no relaxed threshold. Tried SECOND in decision_order.
hypotheses.caveat: joint_necessity reads on-disk seeds; libxml2 lives on server B which arbitrates it. 'xmlns' is a 5-char token, well above the chance-occurrence floor for a 5-byte contiguous string in arbitrary corpus bytes.

hypotheses.label: vpc's resolving seeds drive the attribute-namespace equality chain measurably DEEPER per seed than value_profile's blocking seeds, independent of raw byte alignment

arm_repointing.note: depth_reach runs the winner-resolving (vpc) vs loser-blocking (vp) seed sets through the libafl-libxml2-cov image and reads the SAX2.c:1171 branch line's per-seed-normalized execution count + blocked(resolving)-side count. The discriminating comparison is winner_depth (vpc reaches/executes the equality chain more per seed and lands its resolving side) vs loser_depth (vp seeds reach the line but never its resolving side).

hypotheses.prediction: G1 (genuinely DIFFERENT measurement than ALL three content tests above -- an EXECUTION-DEPTH count via instrumented replay, NOT corpus byte content): the three content tests (byte_freq_ratio / value_distance / token_count) all FAILED for 6193 because the gate's 6-byte operand materializes in a DERIVED name[] buffer (xmlSplitQName output), so it is not where any raw-input-content measurement looks. depth_reach sidesteps content entirely: it replays seeds and reads the cov binary's count at the SAX2.c:1171 equality chain. i2s-pro predicts vpc's seeds, carrying spliced 'xmlns'-bearing attribute names, drive the equality chain and land its RESOLVING side (side_lift high, winner_deeper=true), while vp's seeds reach the line but its decoded name never equals 'xmlns' so they only take the non-resolving side (loser_depth reaches but side_lift positive). DISCRIMINATED ALTERNATIVE: if value_profile actually assembles equivalent 'xmlns' attribute names, vp seeds would land the resolving side just as often -> side_lift ~ 0, winner_deeper=false, REFUTING the gradient-blind story. depth_reach reads the SAME side-count signal independent of where in raw bytes the value sits, which is exactly the model the failed content tests could not provide.
hypotheses.novelty_note: FOURTH, genuinely-new mechanism+measurement for the rule_not_met branch 6193, using a BUILT-but-not-yet-tried-here tool (depth_reach). NOT a relaxed re-run: the three prior tests all measured CORPUS BYTE CONTENT (frequency / distance / token presence) and all missed the value because it lives in a derived name[] buffer; depth_reach measures the INSTRUMENTED EXECUTION outcome at the gate line via cov-binary replay, the only model that does not depend on locating the value in raw input. Placed FIRST in decision_order as the primary discriminator for 6193.
hypotheses.caveat: depth_reach is Docker per-branch (~30-60s) and needs the libafl-libxml2-cov image; libxml2 is server B's target, so sB arbitrates. side_lift threshold 1.5 chosen for a clean resolving-side split (vp seeds reach the line but should rarely take its resolving side).

hypotheses.label: vpc assembles a LARGER namespaced start-tag structure (and drives the empty-element close deeper) than value_profile reaches; vp's gradient cannot synthesize the dispersed multibyte tag

arm_repointing.note: 9435 is a state_or_grammar / dispersed_multibyte STRUCTURAL gate (parser.c:11687 ctxt->nsNr - nsNr > 0, reached only after xmlParseStartTag2 builds a namespaced start tag AND a '/>' empty-element close). The literal is NOT contiguous in raw input (dispersed across the tag) so byte_freq_ratio / value_distance / token_count cannot target it (data_realities: joint/structural gates have different-sized W vs L seeds, byte-alignment fails). depth_reach replays vpc-resolving vs vp-blocking seeds and reads the parser.c:11687 line's per-seed-normalized exec count + resolving(nsNr>0)-side count: winner_deeper/side_lift discriminate whether vpc reaches and TAKES the namespaced-empty-element path that vp's seeds stall short of (the cards show vp seeds take the false branch T=0 F=58 at 11687 and never reach nsPop at 11688).

hypotheses.prediction: G1 (a STRUCTURAL-DEPTH execution measurement, distinct from every content test and matched to a dispersed_multibyte structural gate where byte-alignment is forbidden by data_realities): i2s-pro predicts vpc's resolving seeds -- carrying the I2S-spliced xmlns-bearing start tag and '/>' close -- reach parser.c:11687 AND take its resolving side (ctxt->nsNr - nsNr > 0, the namespaced empty-element path, reaching nsPop at 11688), so side_lift is high and winner_deeper=true, while vp's blocking seeds stall in DTD/declaration text and take the false branch (the card's T=0 F=58 at 11687). DISCRIMINATED ALTERNATIVE: if value_profile's gradient could also assemble a closed namespaced empty element, vp seeds would take the resolving side too -> side_lift ~ 0, winner_deeper=false, REFUTING the I2S-structural-assembly story.
hypotheses.novelty_note: NEW mechanism+measurement for the rule_not_met branch 9435, using depth_reach (BUILT, not yet tried on this shape). 9435 is a structural namespace-assembly gate with a DISPERSED multibyte literal and different-sized W/L seeds, so per data_realities byte-aligned content tools are forbidden -- the prior round's content tests genuinely could not target it. depth_reach measures the structural execution outcome (does vpc TAKE the namespaced-empty-element resolving side that vp stalls before), the matched measurement model for an assembly-depth gate. Placed FIRST in 9435's decision_order.
hypotheses.caveat: depth_reach is Docker per-branch and needs the libafl-libxml2-cov image; sB arbitrates libxml2. depth_reach's scope note flags it is BLIND to ctx context-reach subtypes (no per-call-context counters), but 9435 is an ordinary structural side-count, which the standard cov binary DOES capture, so it is in scope.

decidable_false.reason: 8138 is a single_byte high-bit/IS_CHAR decode gate (parserInternals.c:764, if (!IS_CHAR(val))) with operand_literal=null and operand_width_bytes=null. The branch-deciding value `val` is PRODUCED BY multibyte-UTF-8 DECODING of the cursor bytes (e.g. a 0xf5/0xff lead run), NOT a constant operand present anywhere in the input. There is therefore NO corpus-content signature any built content tool can target: byte_freq_ratio/value_distance/token_count all need a fixed operand value (none exists -- it is decode-derived); and a depth_reach side-count at parserInternals.c:764 would NOT discriminate I2S from VP either, because reaching the TRUE side merely requires ANY high-bit byte run that decodes out of IS_CHAR range -- value_profile's havoc can flip the high bit just as readily as I2S can splice a comparand, so the resolving-side outcome is not I2S-specific (no falsifiable+discriminating prediction separating the two arms). This is an HONEST non-discriminable verdict (G3), not a data gap: the mechanism distinction (I2S-splice vs VP-flip producing an out-of-range decoded codepoint) leaves no signature distinguishable by any of the 5 built measurement tools. If a future tool measured per-byte high-bit-run enrichment in the corpus AND could attribute it to I2S vs havoc, 8138 could be revisited -- but no such attributable tool exists (and I2S is not lineage-tagged, so an 'I2S event' descriptor is infeasible by data_realities).
decidable_false.note: Round-2's seed_starved framing for 8138 is SUPERSEDED: the brief reports 8138 was actually scored (1/3 rules tested) on sB, so seeds exist -- the issue is non-discriminability of the mechanism, not missing data. Carried by fallback:inconclusive with decidable:false.

data_realities_respected: On-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 on s4; all 3 branches here are libxml2, which lives on server B. Every seed/corpus/cov-replay measurement (operand_enrichment, value_distance_reached, joint_necessity/token_count, depth_reach) is scored by the OWNING server (sB), NOT marked decidable:false here for a corpus absent on the running server (cross-server rule 4).

I2S is NOT lineage-tagged and value_profile is a feedback not a mutator: NONE of the hypotheses use seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an 'I2S event' or a 'gradient event'. The new depth_reach hypotheses read INSTRUMENTED EXECUTION side-counts from cov-binary replay (a content-independent outcome signal), and the content hypotheses read corpus byte content -- never a lineage event. This is also why 8138 is decidable:false: its only conceivable attribution (I2S-splice vs VP-flip of a high bit) WOULD require an I2S lineage event, which is infeasible.

joint / structural-assembly gates have different-SIZED winning vs blocking seeds, so byte-alignment (byte_freq_ratio) fails -- this is precisely why 9435 (state_or_grammar / dispersed_multibyte) is routed to depth_reach (an execution side-count, alignment-free) and excluded from all byte/distance content tests.

depth_reach is Docker per-branch (~30-60s) and needs the target's libafl-<target>-cov image, and is BLIND to the ctx context-reach subtype (standard cov binary has no per-call-context counters). Both new depth_reach hypotheses (6193 SAX2.c:1171; 9435 parser.c:11687) read ORDINARY branch-side counts, which the standard cov binary DOES capture, so both are in scope; neither is a per-call-context counter.

operand_enrichment skips structural/length gates and needs a fixed-offset VALUE gate with a contiguous literal at a STABLE offset; for 6193 the gate compares a DERIVED name[] buffer (xmlSplitQName output) with no stable input offset, which is why the fixed-offset enrichment rule did not hold and why the prior content tests are retained UNCHANGED (carry-forward) rather than re-thresholded, with depth_reach added as the offset-independent primary.

notes.priors_kept: KEPT ALL THREE prior hypotheses verbatim per the superset/monotonic rule: i2s_multibyte_literal_splice_vp_gradient_blind (operand_enrichment), i2s_lands_literal_vp_gradient_stalls_distance (value_distance_reached), and i2s_xmlns_token_present_vp_corpus_lacks (joint_necessity/token_count). All three are marked confirmed >=1 branch in the re-invoke brief (KEEP). Rules unchanged, no relaxed thresholds, so any server / future libxml2 corpus re-validates the confirmed branch.
notes.new_mechanisms: ADDED TWO genuinely-new hypotheses, both using depth_reach (BUILT tool, NOT yet tried on this shape per the brief): (1) i2s_attr_namespace_parse_depth_per_seed for 6193 -- measures the SAX2.c:1171 equality-chain RESOLVING-side execution count per seed via cov-binary replay, the offset-independent model the three failed content tests could not provide (the value lives in a derived name[] buffer). (2) i2s_namespace_starttag_structure_assembly for 9435 -- measures the parser.c:11687 namespaced-empty-element resolving-side count, the matched model for a dispersed_multibyte structural-assembly gate where byte-alignment is forbidden by data_realities.
notes.why_genuinely_different: All three priors measure CORPUS BYTE CONTENT: byte_freq_ratio = frequency at a FIXED offset (failed: no stable offset); value_distance_reached = Hamming distance over SLIDING WINDOWS of raw input (failed: value materializes in a derived buffer); token_count = OCCURRENCE of the contiguous token in raw bytes (failed: the relevant literal is dispersed/derived, not a clean raw token). The two new hypotheses measure INSTRUMENTED EXECUTION side-counts at the gate line via cov-binary replay (depth_reach) -- a content-INDEPENDENT outcome signal that does not require locating the value in raw input. Different metric family over a different measurement model, not a threshold relaxation.
notes.decidable_false_subgroup: ONE branch marked permanently decidable:false (G3, honest inconclusive): libxml2_8138. It is a single_byte IS_CHAR decode gate with operand_literal=null -- the branch-deciding `val` is DECODE-DERIVED (produced by UTF-8 decoding of a high-bit byte run), not a constant operand. No built content tool has a target value to measure, and a depth_reach side-count would NOT discriminate I2S from VP because ANY high-bit run (havoc-flippable by value_profile, not just I2S-spliceable) decodes out of IS_CHAR range -- the resolving-side outcome is not I2S-specific, so there is no falsifiable+discriminating prediction separating the arms. The only conceivable attribution (I2S-splice vs VP-flip) would require an I2S lineage event, which is infeasible (I2S not lineage-tagged). Round-2's seed_starved framing for 8138 is SUPERSEDED (the brief reports it WAS scored, so seeds exist; the issue is mechanism non-discriminability, not missing data).
notes.g1_novelty: Both new hypotheses are falsifiable (side_lift could come out ~0, winner_deeper=false) and discriminating (if value_profile assembled equivalent xmlns attribute names / namespaced empty elements, vp seeds would take the resolving side just as often, refuting the I2S-assembly story). Neither is a relaxed-threshold re-run of any refuted prior -- a different metric family (execution side-count) over a different measurement model (cov-binary replay, content-independent).
notes.g2_independence: depth_reach reads the gate line's execution side-count from REPLAY of the (already-saved) seed sets through the cov binary -- this is independent of the campaign's resolve PATTERN that defined the shape (which trials vpc resolved vs vp blocked at final checkpoint). The seed sets are inputs; the measured side-split is computed fresh by replay, so it is not verifying-vpc-resolves-with-vpc-resolves. The content priors likewise read corpus content, independent of the resolve pattern.
notes.collapse_rationale: All 3 branches share the I2S-exact-sequence/structure-vs-VP-gradient-blind story but do NOT collapse into one scorable test: 6193 (contiguous derived literal) and 9435 (dispersed structural literal) each get a depth_reach primary matched to their gate kind, plus 6193 retains the three content priors. 8138 (single_byte, null operand, decode-derived value) shares neither a stable operand nor an I2S-discriminable side-count, so it cannot share a measurement and is honestly decidable:false.
